subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Region R — Area, Rotation about y=4 & Square Cross-Section Volume (Write Only)
stimulus: Let $f(x) = 2x^2 - 6x + 4$ and $g(x) = 4\cos\left(\frac{\pi x}{4}\right)$. Let $R$ be the region bounded by the graphs of $f$ and $g$, as shown in the figure.

rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct limits: $x = 0$ and $x = 2$ (intersection points of $f$ and $g$)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Area $= \int_0^2 \left[4\cos\left(\frac{\pi x}{4}\right) - (2x^2-6x+4)\right]\, dx = \left[\frac{16}{\pi}\sin\frac{\pi x}{4} - \frac{2x^3}{3}+3x^2-4x\right]_0^2 = \frac{16}{\pi} - \frac{4}{3}$.

parts.prompt: Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the volume of the solid generated when $R$ is rotated about the horizontal line $y = 4$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Uses washer method with correct limits ($0$ to $2$) and constant $\pi$
1 pt: Correct outer radius: $4 - f(x) = 4 - (2x^2-6x+4) = -2x^2+6x$; correct inner radius: $4 - g(x) = 4 - 4\cos(\frac{\pi x}{4})$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \pi\int_0^2 \left[(4-f(x))^2 - (4-g(x))^2\right]\, dx$ (do not evaluate).

parts.prompt: The region $R$ is the base of a solid. For this solid, each cross section perpendicular to the $x$-axis is a square. Write, but do not evaluate, an integral expression that gives the volume of the solid.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct expression with limits: $\int_0^2 \left[g(x) - f(x)\right]^2\, dx$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Volume $= \int_0^2 \left[4\cos\left(\frac{\pi x}{4}\right) - (2x^2-6x+4)\right]^2\, dx$ (do not evaluate).
